picks.reason: Ray's disastrous recent form (L3 ERA 10.22, xERA 5.18) and high barrel rate clash with Cabrera's more stable underlying numbers. Moderate drizzle and 63% rain chance at Wrigley adds variance that prevents a clean 4%+ edge. Market at -160 sits inside my fair line of -175 for CHC.

Luzardo's excellent xERA (3.16) and K-BB profile top Kay's inflated underlying metrics, yet PHI's taxed bullpen (Alvarado and Kerkering used yesterday) offsets the edge. CHW's 7-3 last-10 form is a legitimate counter. Estimated fair line -172 versus market -185 is too close.

Woo's elite xERA (2.91), low barrel rate, and strong last-three starts (2.04 ERA) create a clear pitching advantage over Valdez (xERA 4.38, L3 ERA 4.58). SEA also owns the better recent form (8-2 last 10) and positive run differential. One piece of evidence against is Detroit's home-field advantage. Fair line -162 versus market -134 produces >5% edge with fresh bullpens on both sides.

NYY's superior run differential (+93) and home offense favor them, but Gray's xERA (4.38) is worse than his surface ERA and NYY's bullpen has multiple arms on consecutive days. Market -143 is inside my fair line of -155. No qualifying inefficiency after weighing both bullpen risks.

TOR's bullpen is severely taxed (Chad Dallas 68 pitches yesterday plus multiple arms on consecutive days), creating late-inning vulnerability despite Yesavage's strong underlying numbers in limited innings. BAL's 7-3 last-10 form supplies a second independent factor. One piece of evidence against is Yesavage's 2.50 xERA. Fair line near TOR -125 versus market -155 yields actionable value on the dog.

Rasmussen holds a measurable edge in xERA (3.31 vs Gusto's tiny-sample instability), yet TB's poor 2-8 last-10 form and MIA's respectable home record neutralize it. Market at -136 aligns with my fair line of -148. Bullpens are fresh on both sides but no 4%+ gap exists.

ATL's elite team quality and home run differential are clear, but PIT's strong 7-3 last-10 mark and Keller's decent FIP (3.38) keep the game within 4%. ATL bullpen is also taxed. Estimated fair line ATL -158 versus market -142 is too narrow for action.

Perkins' excellent FIP (2.60) and xERA (3.14) in small sample look promising against Lambert's worse recent form, yet both bullpens carry heavy taxation and ATH's overall run differential remains negative. Market near pick'em matches my mental fair line of ATH -112. Edge below 4%.

Singer's terrible 6.88 FIP and 7.82 L3 ERA destroy any CIN edge despite STL's own 3-7 last-10 slide. Leahy's home splits and bullpen freshness further support STL. My fair line of STL -150 is close to market -139. No bet.

Messick's 2.21 ERA and 3.04 FIP stand out, but CLE's bullpen is taxed and TEX owns a slightly better run differential at home. Market -137 sits inside my fair line of -152. The bullpen risk prevents clearing the 4% threshold.

MIN's bullpen is extremely taxed across multiple arms on consecutive days, yet KC's own poor form (3-7 last 10) and negative run differential make trusting them at -102 impossible. Wacha versus Matthews is close once small-sample caution is applied. No edge.

Coors Field and two mediocre starters (Sproat 5.59 FIP, Feltner 6.32 xERA) point toward high scoring, but MIL's taxed bullpen (Woodford 56p, Anderson 30p) and COL's home underdogs status create too many conflicting variables. Fair line near MIL -140 is too close to -152.

Kelly's horrific xERA (7.26) and 16.4% barrel rate signal major regression risk against Griffin's more stable 4.41 xERA and solid road performance. WAS owns a strong 19-12 away record as a second independent factor. One piece of evidence against is Kelly's recent 3.44 L3 ERA. Market -134 is 15+ cents off my fair line of AZ -112.

Scott's small-sample promise and King's steady 3.84 FIP balance each other, while SD's 1-9 last-10 slump is offset by their home bullpen advantage (Mason Miller available). Estimated fair line SD -128 versus market -132 is inside the no-edge window.

LAD's massive +133 run differential and Sasaki's recent dominance are real, yet LAD's taxed bullpen (Vesia, Hurt, Scott all used yesterday) and LAA's decent last-10 mark reduce the edge on a heavy favorite. Fair line near -175 sits close to -195. No plus-money RL edge after risk adjustment.

parlay.reason: Both legs cleared the 4% edge threshold independently on pitching xERA advantages and team form; games occur in different leagues, time zones, and weather patterns with zero correlation.

best_bet.why_this: Woo's superior xERA, barrel suppression, and SEA's 8-2 last-10 form create the slate's largest probability gap versus market price, with fresh bullpens on both sides removing the risk factor present in every other candidate.
